current_state.onboarding_tour: Current implementation uses driver.js library with 4 tooltip steps that highlight sidebar elements (#tour-setores, #tour-colaboradores, #tour-contratos). It's a shallow tour that only points to menu items without explaining the actual workflow or business logic.
current_state.driver_js_usage: driver.js is imported in OnboardingTour.tsx (lines 2-3), configured with custom options (Portuguese text, dark mode CSS class), and triggered automatically on first visit or manually via event listener 'escalaflow:open-onboarding'.
current_state.sidebar_como_funciona: Located in AppSidebar.tsx footer dropdown menu (lines 239-247). Currently removes localStorage flag and dispatches custom event 'escalaflow:open-onboarding' which OnboardingTour listens to. Menu item is in DropdownMenu with HelpCircle icon.
current_state.css_driver_block: Lines 86-164 in src/renderer/src/index.css. Contains custom CSS for .driver-popover.escalaflow-tour-popover with dark mode overrides using Tailwind CSS variables (hsl(var(--popover)), etc.).

patterns.page_component_convention: Pages follow consistent structure: (1) Imports from lucide-react, shadcn/ui, custom components, services, hooks. (2) useState for local UI state. (3) useApiData hook for data fetching. (4) Loading state check with early return. (5) Render with PageHeader + breadcrumbs + actions, main content area with flex-1 and p-6 spacing. (6) Dialogs/modals for actions. (7) Forms use react-hook-form + Zod validation.
patterns.routing_pattern: React Router v7 with HashRouter (Electron compatible). Routes defined in App.tsx with <Route path='...' element={<Component />} />. All routes wrapped in SidebarProvider > AppSidebar + SidebarInset layout. Navigation via <Link to='...'> or useNavigate() hook.
patterns.dark_mode_setup: Uses next-themes ThemeProvider (main.tsx line 12) with attribute='class', defaultTheme='system', storageKey='escalaflow-theme'. Tailwind configured with darkMode: ['class']. CSS variables defined in :root and .dark in index.css (lines 6-75). Theme switcher available in sidebar dropdown menu with Light/Dark/System options.
patterns.localStorage_pattern: Simple key-value storage. Keys use 'escalaflow-' prefix (e.g., 'escalaflow-onboarding-v1', 'escalaflow-theme'). ViewToggle component stores view mode per-page (e.g., 'escalaflow-view-setores'). No complex serialization - mostly boolean flags ('1' for true) or simple strings.

files_to_create.purpose: New full-page onboarding experience with 6 steps, stepper navigation, visual content explaining the complete workflow (Setores > Demandas > Colaboradores > Gerar Escala > Ajustar > Oficializar). Replaces tooltip-based tour.

files_to_modify.changes: 1. Add route <Route path='/bem-vindo' element={<BemVindo />} />. 2. Remove import and <OnboardingTour /> component (lines 4, 20). 3. Add redirect logic: if localStorage.getItem('escalaflow-onboarding-v1') is null and path is '/', redirect to '/bem-vindo' (can be done via useEffect in App or wrapper component).

1. Remove tourId attributes from mainNav and configNav arrays (lines 50-57). 2. Remove id={item.tourId} from SidebarMenuItem (lines 116, 142). 3. Replace 'Como Funciona?' onClick handler (lines 239-247) to navigate to '/bem-vindo' instead of localStorage.removeItem + event dispatch. Change to: <Link to='/bem-vindo'>. 4. Remove event listener code entirely.
Delete lines 86-164 (entire driver.js CSS block starting with /* driver.js tour — dark mode overrides */ and ending with last .driver-popover.escalaflow-tour-popover rule).
Remove 'driver.js' from dependencies (currently line 44: "driver.js": "^1.4.0"). Run npm uninstall driver.js after file modification.

files_to_delete.reason: Entire component replaced by BemVindo.tsx full-page experience. No longer needed as driver.js tooltip tour is being removed.

risks: Redirect logic from '/' to '/bem-vindo' might cause loop if not implemented correctly (need to check localStorage BEFORE redirect).
Need to ensure 'Como Funciona?' link works both when localStorage flag exists (navigates to page) and when it doesn't (redirect already happened).
CSS removal might affect other components if driver.js classes leaked elsewhere (low risk, grep shows no other usage).
Custom stepper component needs to be responsive and work well with both sidebar states (open/collapsed).
All 6 steps need visual polish - icons, spacing, typography - to match existing design system quality.
Need to verify dark mode works perfectly on all 6 steps (new page, not tested yet).
notes: BemVindo.tsx should stay INSIDE SidebarProvider layout (not full-screen) so users see the sidebar mentioned in the tour steps.
Stepper component needs to be custom (shadcn/ui doesn't have native Stepper) - can use Badge for step numbers, lucide-react icons for visual markers.
Content should be simple, non-technical language targeting non-tech HR users (parents of Marco).

The 6 steps from PRD: (1) Welcome + value prop, (2) Setores explanation, (3) Demandas explanation, (4) Colaboradores + contracts + exceptions, (5) Generate flow (gerar -> ajustar -> oficializar), (6) Ready + CTA to Dashboard.
Navigation buttons: Anterior (disabled on step 1), Proximo (changes to 'Comecar' on step 6), Pular Tour (always visible, sets flag and navigates).
Progress indicator can be simple: 'Passo X de 6' text + visual stepper with numbered circles.
localStorage key remains 'escalaflow-onboarding-v1' (same as current implementation).
Event 'escalaflow:open-onboarding' can be removed entirely - no longer needed since navigation is via Link.
Consider adding fade-in transition animation using Tailwind animate classes for step content changes.
All existing pages already have loading states pattern - BemVindo doesn't need API calls, just localStorage and navigate.
